content:
Thunderbird uses mbox files, not EML file so I guess you should probably import your EML files with the importexport addon. https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/importexporttools-ng/?src=ss

Thunderbird can export an EML file and if it is double clicked on can open and display the contents, but natively it does not use to format.

content:
Your first screen shot shows the contents of a POP account folder in the jtqu7154.Dwain profile folder in the correct default location ..../Users/Dwain/Library/Thunderbird/Profiles/jtqu7154.Dwain/Mail/... In your second and third screen shots a profile folder having the same name jtqu7154.Dwain is located directly in ..../Users/Dwain/Library/jtqu7154.Dwain/ Thunderbird doesn't read a profile folder in your user library!

It's up to you to find out in which of the two profiles the missing subfolder files are located. Also remember that in Thunderbird a folder with subfolders is represented in the profile by a "Folder.sbd" file folder and "Folder" mailbox file having the same name, without extension. In your third screen shot several of those mailbox files are missing, e.g. Community, Current Work, Festivals, Previous, 2009, 2010, 2011, ...... etc If in the profile folder an *.sbd file folder is not "accompanied" by a mailbox file having the same name, Thunderbird won't display neither the folder nor its subfolders.
creator: Mapenzi

content:
No, what you have done accidentally is to move your jtqu7154.Dwain profile folder also directly in your user library. And your new screen shot again shows contents of the "wrong" profile folder.

You have two jtqu7154.Dwain profile folders: one is present in its default location in System Disk > Users > Dwain > Library > Profiles > jtqu7154.Dwain and the second is located in Disk > Users > Dwain > Library > jtqu7154.Dwain So it's no use to show me again the contents of the same jtqu7154.Dwain profile folder which is not in the right place!

Please return to your very first screen shot and follow the path System Disk > Users > Dwain > Library > Profiles > jtqu7154.Dwain > Mail > and make a screen shot of everything beyond Mail. If possible activate the "Columns" view in your Finder since it will be easier for me verify if certain mailbox files are missing

content:
I prefer your new screen shot! It shows that a lot of mailbox (without extension) files corresponding to .sbd folders are missing. As I already wrote in my first response: a folder with subfolders in Thunderbird is represented in the profile by a "Folder.sbd" file folder and "Folder" mailbox file having the same name, without extension. I don't know why these mailbox (also called mbox) files have disappeared from your profile. Maybe you have been using antivirus software are some "cleaning tool" which have the bad habit to remove mbox files of 0 kB size.

Now we have to recreate those missing mbox files manually. First open your Thunderbird jtqu7154.Dwain profile folder, navigate to Festivals.sbd and quit Thunderbird via ⌘ + Q. We will first create an empty text file and name it "Previous". To do this open TextEdit, go to menu TextEdit > Preferences and select "Text Format" (image). Then save the empty file on your desktop as "Previous" , delete the suffixe .txt your Mac will add to the file name and move the file in the "Festivals.sbd" folder. All corresponding mbox files for 2009.sbd up to 2026.sbd and NDS.sbd are missing too. So you'll have to repeat this manipulation with TexEdit to create empty mbox files from 2009 to 2026.... plus NDS and move them in Previous.sbd Eventually restart Thunderbird and verify if the folder Previous and its subfolders are present.
creator: Mapenzi

content:
If all you want to save are the email messages, do this:

  1. If IMAP, click File>offline>download first to ensure all messages are there on PC
  2. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  3. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  4. exit thunderbird
  5. click the imapmail folder for IMAP account, or the mail folder for POP account
  6. now, click the account folder
  7. now, look for your message folders there, files such as inbox, inbox.msf, sent, sent.msf, etc. COPY the files (other than the msf ones) to wherever you want to save them.
creator: davidsk
